expectations.text: Ran the bundled analyze_recording.py script on the recording
expectations.evidence: Script output saved to script_output.txt, analysis references script findings throughout

expectations.text: Identified echo/duplication in the system audio track via autocorrelation
expectations.evidence: Clearly stated 'autocorrelation peaks at consistent lags in the 20-53ms range' in the system track with r=0.4752 at 53ms and r=0.3412 at 20.9ms

expectations.text: Reported the approximate echo lag (30-53ms range)
expectations.evidence: Reported exact values: 53.0ms (r=0.4752), 20.9ms (r=0.3412), 31.2ms (r=0.3028). Stated 'Median echo delay: ~31ms'

expectations.text: Noted that cross-track correlation is near zero (not mic bleed)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly stated: 'Cross-correlation between system and mic tracks is near zero at all time points (max 0.0164), and voice-band coherence is 0.0000. This means the mic is NOT picking up system audio through the speakers.'

expectations.text: Flagged that AEC-processed tracks are over-amplified
expectations.evidence: Stated: 'aec_mic: -8.5 LUFS (was -18.6, now WAY too loud)' and 'LOUDNESS BLOWN OUT'
Reported both: STOI 0.540-0.582 (poor), PESQ WB degrades from 4.10 to 2.49 over recording

expectations.text: Provided actionable explanation of echo cause
expectations.evidence: Identified virtual audio processor as likely cause, recommended investigating Krisp/Loopback, advised not using processed file, suggested comb-filter approach
